title: Scheduling, Affinity, Taints, Topology, Priority, Preemption, and Eviction · Cluster Repair Bay
summary: An incident responder inherits a plausible but defective placement and disruption decision record; preserve status and events, isolate the first causal fault in scheduling, affinity, taints, topology, priority, preemption, and eviction, and retain a regression check. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in placement and disruption decision record and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pract…
objectives: Use trusted node labels, nodeSelector, required and preferred node affinity, operators, weights, and ignored-during-execution semantics.
Design inter-Pod affinity and anti-affinity with selectors, namespaces, topology keys, scheduler cost, and failure-domain consequences.
Trace NoSchedule, PreferNoSchedule, and NoExecute taints, matching tolerations, toleration time, node conditions, and admission-added behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Priority Preemption Eviction
steps.instruction:
Append the Kubernetes evidence item headed "  # Evidence: k8s-priority-preemption-eviction-iy7ybk". Tailor its placement-policy.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic API or manifest review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run kubectl, Helm, kubeadm, and cluster commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Priority Preemption Eviction.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns K8s Priority Preemption Eviction into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Priority Preemption Eviction, keep K8s Priority Preemption Eviction connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-priority-preemption-eviction-iy7ybk
  - competency: k8s-priority-preemption-eviction
    artifact: placement-policy.yaml
    decision: priority preemption eviction
    environment: air-gapped release cluster using an internal registry mirror
    fault-injection: restore a claim in a different topology and numeric identity
    retained-proof: EndpointSlice DNS policy route and packet-path correlation
    case-key: iy7ybk
    change: |
      node-affinity: trusted-workload-class
      toleration: dedicated=intake:NoSchedule
      topologySpread: zones-maxSkew-1
      priorityClass: intake-critical
      preemption-policy: evidence-gated
      eviction: graceful-and-budgeted
    verify: kubectl get pod status-api -o wide && kubectl get events --field-selector involvedObject.name=status-api
    failure: Reject toleration-as-attraction, preferred-as-required, replica count as spread proof, or priority as permission for unbounded disruption.
    repair: Derive eligible nodes and domains, calculate skew, inspect unschedulable reasons, separate preemption from eviction, and preserve graceful recovery.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate scheduling priority, preemption, nomination, PodDisruptionBudget interaction, node-pressure eviction, API eviction, graceful termination, and fairness.

hints: For Publish the incident record · Debug K8s Priority Preemption Eviction, name the context, namespace, API identity, desired state, observed status, controller, policy, and failure-domain boundary, K8s Priority Preemption Eviction precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Publish the incident record · Debug K8s Priority Preemption Eviction, trace this concrete input and predict admission, defaulting, scheduling, reconciliation, status, traffic, storage, policy, disruption, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A debug Pod is Pending with an untolerated taint, required anti-affinity, insufficient memory, and topology spread skew 3. Reconstruct filtering, scoring, requests, selectors, affinity, taints, preemption, and eviction without forcing nodeName. Competency probe: Separate scheduling priority, preemption, nomination, PodDisruptionBudget interaction, node-pressure eviction, API eviction, graceful termination, and fairness.

For Publish the incident record · Debug K8s Priority Preemption Eviction, challenge the documented misconception with object, condition, event, policy, failure, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: A higher priority Pod immediately and safely evicts any lower priority Pod until it runs.
